skill.name: blockchain-developer
skill.description: Guides development of Web3 applications, smart contracts, DeFi protocols, NFT platforms, DAOs, and enterprise blockchain integrations.
skill.summary: Build secure Web3 applications, smart contracts, and decentralized systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, sensitive key terminology, Cobalt Strike wording, weak cryptography keywords, and reconnaissance wording. Manual review found these are false positives in documentation prose, with no executable code, prompt injection, or exfiltration behavior found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Finding Is Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 26 contains a Markdown path in backticks instructing the assistant to open an optional resource file, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is Markdown formatting around a resource path. I found no shell command, subprocess call, script, or executable file in the provided file structure.

low_findings.title: Cobalt Strike Keyword Finding Is Blockchain Terminology
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 43 uses the phrase beacon proxies, which is a smart contract upgrade pattern and not Cobalt Strike behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section discusses upgradeable contract patterns. No command-and-control, malware, payload, listener, or operator workflow evidence appears in context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Substring Matches In Prose
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe blockchain topics, user experience, gas optimization, compatibility, and governance design, not weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic implementation. The flagged locations are plain-language blockchain capability descriptions.

low_findings.title: Private Key Finding Is Security Guidance Context
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 153 mentions private key management with hardware wallet integration as a defensive security topic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a security and auditing best practices section. I found no instruction to collect, expose, transmit, or misuse private keys.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Security Priority Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 162 states that the skill prioritizes security and formal verification, not host reconnaissance or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a behavioral trait in prose. There are no commands, APIs, or instructions for enumerating files, users, networks, or system configuration.

content.user_title: Build Secure Web3 Contracts and Apps
content.value_statement: Blockchain projects need secure architecture, audited contracts, and clear deployment plans. This skill helps design Web3 systems with smart contracts, dApps, and protocol workflows.
actual_capabilities: Plans smart contract architecture for Ethereum and EVM-compatible networks.
Explains DeFi, NFT, DAO, bridge, and tokenomics design patterns.
Reviews smart contract risks such as reentrancy, overflow, and access control.
Guides Web3 frontend integration with wallets, libraries, and onboarding flows.
Outlines deployment, monitoring, indexing, and testnet workflows.
Documents audit-ready behavior, assumptions, and security checks.
limitations: It does not replace an independent smart contract audit.
It cannot guarantee legal, tax, or regulatory compliance.
It needs project-specific chain, token, and custody requirements.
It may need current ecosystem verification for recent protocol changes.

use_cases.title: Design a smart contract system
use_cases.description: Create a contract architecture with roles, upgrade paths, tests, and deployment steps.

use_cases.title: Plan a Web3 product build
use_cases.description: Define wallet flows, chain selection, token standards, indexing, and user onboarding.

use_cases.title: Prepare for security review
use_cases.description: Identify common smart contract risks and create an audit-ready checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a blockchain stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a blockchain stack for [project]. Compare networks, wallet options, contract standards, and deployment complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Design a contract architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the smart contract architecture for [protocol]. Include roles, state changes, upgrade strategy, events, and testing priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Review a DeFi mechanism
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this DeFi mechanism: [description]. Identify economic risks, oracle assumptions, liquidation logic, and abuse cases.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an audit package
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an audit preparation plan for [contracts]. Include threat model, invariants, test coverage, deployment controls, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Design an NFT marketplace for creators.
output_examples.output: A marketplace plan covering token standards, royalty handling, custody choices, metadata storage, listing flows, and contract review checkpoints.
A risk review covering collateral rules, oracle dependency, liquidation incentives, governance controls, and required simulation tests.
A launch checklist covering testnet validation, multisig setup, deployment verification, monitoring, incident response, and user communication.

best_practices: Define threat models before writing contracts.
Use audited libraries and document every trust assumption.
Test invariants, edge cases, upgrades, and emergency controls.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy unaudited contracts that control user funds.
Do not store private keys, seed phrases, or secrets in prompts.
Do not rely on a single oracle or administrator without safeguards.

faq.question: Can this skill write smart contract plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline architecture, roles, upgrade patterns, tests, and deployment steps.

faq.question: Can it replace a professional audit?
faq.answer: No. It supports preparation and review, but independent auditors should assess production contracts.

faq.question: Which ecosystems does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Ethereum, EVM networks, Solana, Cosmos, Polkadot, NEAR, Cardano, Algorand, and Hyperledger concepts.

faq.question: Can it help with Web3 frontend work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan wallet integration, dApp flows, authentication, indexing, and onboarding.

faq.question: Does it handle private keys?
faq.answer: No. Users should never provide private keys or seed phrases in prompts.

faq.question: Is it useful for enterprise blockchain projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes permissioned networks, identity, asset tokenization, compliance planning, and integration patterns.
